title: Aggregations like count(), max() return zero values for Distributed tables with enable_parallel_replicas=1
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
SELECT max(col_name) FROM DB.table;
returns zero value (1970-01-01 for DateTime) for Distributed tables on 3-node replicated cluster with allow_experimental_parallel_reading_from_replicas=1 setting.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
- version 25.8.10 official build
- 25.8 and 25.9 seem to be affected, 25.7.8.71 works as expected
- 3-node cluster with replication and allow_experimental_parallel_reading_from_replicas=1
```SQL
CREATE TABLE TestDB.test_repl on cluster cluster
(
`time_col` DateTime
)
ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree('/clickhouse/tables/{shard}/TestDB/test_repl-{uuid}', '{replica}')
PARTITION BY tuple()
ORDER BY time_col;
create table TestDB.test on cluster cluster
as TestDB.test_repl
engine = Distributed('cluster', 'TestDB', 'test_repl', rand());
insert into TestDB.test (time_col) values (now());
insert into TestDB.test (time_col) values (now());
select count(), max(time_col) from TestDB.test;
```
produces
0, 1970-01-01 00:00:00
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
With
SETTINGS enable_parallel_replicas=0 or optimize_use_implicit_projections=0
results are correct
SELECT max(toDateTime(time_col)) ...
SELECT countIf(...)
SELECT max(time_col) FROM TestDB.table WHERE time_col>1;
return correct results too
poc:
CREATE TABLE TestDB.test_repl on cluster cluster
(
    `time_col` DateTime
)
    ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree('/clickhouse/tables/{shard}/TestDB/test_repl-{uuid}', '{replica}')
        PARTITION BY tuple()
        ORDER BY time_col;
key_bug_features: Aggregations return zero values for Distributed tables
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE TestDB.test_repl on cluster cluster (id UInt32, time_col DateTime, value Float64) ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree('/clickhouse/tables/{shard}/TestDB/test_repl-{uuid}', '{replica}') PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(time_col) ORDER BY (id, time_col);
CREATE TABLE TestDB.test on cluster cluster AS TestDB.test_repl ENGINE = Distributed('cluster', 'TestDB', 'test_repl', rand());
INSERT INTO TestDB.test (id, time_col, value) VALUES (1, now(), 10.5);
INSERT INTO TestDB.test (id, time_col, value) VALUES (2, now(), NULL);
SELECT count(), max(time_col) FROM TestDB.test WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
